title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
keyDrivers: Much of this role, gathering data, running surveys, analyzing trends, and writing reports, falls squarely in AI's sweet spot. The work is largely desk-based with no physical component, requires no licensure, and the judgment involved often follows well-established frameworks rather than truly novel thinking. While some analysts will shift toward interpreting AI-generated insights, the overall demand for dedicated market research roles is likely to shrink as AI handles the bulk of the analytical work directly.

taskData.full: Collect and analyze data on customer demographics, preferences, needs, and buying habits to identify potential markets and factors affecting product demand.
Conduct research on consumer opinions and marketing strategies, collaborating with marketing professionals, statisticians, pollsters, and other professionals.
Prepare reports of findings, illustrating data graphically and translating complex findings into written text.
Devise and evaluate methods and procedures for collecting data, such as surveys, opinion polls, or questionnaires, or arrange to obtain existing data.
Measure the effectiveness of marketing, advertising, and communications programs and strategies.
Forecast and track marketing and sales trends, analyzing collected data.
Gather data on competitors and analyze their prices, sales, and method of marketing and distribution.
Seek and provide information to help companies determine their position in the marketplace.
Monitor industry statistics and follow trends in trade literature.
Measure and assess customer and employee satisfaction.

risks.body: Competitive intelligence gathering, survey design, and trend forecasting — the core tasks in this role — show AI automation rates above 47% in real usage data, with some tasks like consumer research collaboration running above 57%. Marketing teams are already consolidating headcount: the WEF Future of Jobs 2025 report projects net displacement for data-processing and analytical roles as AI handles the routine research-to-insight pipeline more cheaply [World Economic Forum, Jan 2025]. Lightcast's Global AI Skills Outlook found that AI skill requirements in marketing and PR job postings surged 50% year-over-year — firms are getting more output from fewer, higher-skilled analysts rather than hiring more [Lightcast, May 2025].
risks.statLabel: year-over-year growth in AI skill requirements for marketing roles
risks.statSourceTitle: The Lightcast Global AI Skills Outlook

opportunities.body: The tasks that hold up best are the ones that come after the data — translating findings into specific business decisions, designing the right research questions, and advising stakeholders who can act on results. These judgment-heavy activities show much lower automation pressure. McKinsey's analysis of generative AI's economic potential found that marketing and sales is one of the functions with the highest value from AI augmentation, but the value accrues to practitioners who direct the analysis, not those who run it [McKinsey Global Institute, Jun 2023]. HubSpot's 2025 State of Marketing found that 74% of marketers now use AI tools at work, which is shifting the role's floor: analysts who can pair AI-generated data with qualitative insight and client communication hold ground that AI cannot easily replace [HubSpot, Jan 2025].
opportunities.statLabel: of marketers now use AI tools at work

howToAdapt.alreadyIn: Start by learning one AI-powered research platform end to end. Tools like Qualtrics XM, Remesh, or Discuss.io now handle survey design, data collection, and initial analysis in a fraction of the time it used to take manually. The real value shifts to what happens after the data comes in: synthesizing primary research with cultural trends, behavioral data, and competitive intelligence into recommendations that drive specific business decisions [HubSpot, Jan 2026]. Over six months, build depth in mixed-methods research design and statistical modeling. The researchers pulling ahead are the ones who can tell a client not just what the data says but what to do about it [McKinsey Global Institute, Mar 2025].
howToAdapt.thinkingOf: Entry-level market research is where AI hits hardest. The WEF estimates 39% of workers' core skills will change by 2030, and data collection, basic survey analysis, and report formatting are the first to go [World Economic Forum, Jan 2025]. To stand out, build a portfolio that shows strategic thinking, not just data processing. A certificate in marketing analytics from Google or a CRM platform, combined with a project that demonstrates how research led to a real business decision, signals more than a degree alone. The 33% of marketers already using AI extensively for market research means AI fluency is table stakes, not a differentiator -- differentiate on interpretation and storytelling instead [HubSpot, Jan 2026].

quotes.quote: AI and human marketers generate in tandem, bringing distinct strengths -- structural clarity and strategic framing from AI and contextual judgment and creative nuance from humans. The system makes these strengths explicit, compares alternatives, and empowers the marketer to determine what works best.
quotes.attribution: McKinsey Global Institute, 'AI-powered marketing and sales reach new heights with generative AI'

quotes.quote: Organizations are most often reporting revenue increases from AI use in marketing and sales. But the gains are concentrated in organizations where senior strategists set the research agenda -- AI handles execution, humans handle interpretation and client communication.
quotes.attribution: McKinsey Global Institute, 'The State of AI in 2025'

quotes.quote: 61% of marketers believe the industry is experiencing its biggest disruption in 20 years due to AI -- but the disruption is concentrated in production and data tasks, not in the research design and insight communication work that junior analysts need to build toward.

Analytical and creative thinking remain the top two most in-demand core skills through 2030. Technological skills in AI, big data, and networks are projected to grow fastest -- but human judgment skills are explicitly listed as growing in importance alongside them, not being replaced.
quotes.attribution: World Economic Forum, Future of Jobs Report 2025

sources.name: Lightcast
sources.title: The Lightcast Global AI Skills Outlook

sources.name: McKinsey Global Institute
sources.title: The economic potential of generative AI: The next productivity frontier

sources.name: McKinsey Global Institute
sources.title: AI-powered marketing and sales reach new heights with generative AI

taskIntro: Not all tasks are affected equally. Knowing which ones AI handles well, and which still need a human, is how to focus skill-building.

emergingCareers.title: Consumer Insights Analyst
emergingCareers.description: Uses AI tools to synthesize survey data, social listening, and behavioral analytics into actionable consumer insights reports. Translates AI-generated pattern detection into business recommendations for product and marketing teams.

stat.text: Employment of market research analysts is projected to grow 7% from 2024 to 2034 -- much faster than the average -- with about 87,200 openings projected each year.
stat.sourceTitle: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Market Research Analysts

emergingCareers.fit: Survey design, data synthesis, and report writing carry directly; the shift is from running manual research workflows to curating and validating AI-generated consumer analysis.
steps: Complete Google Data Analytics Certificate on Coursera
Build a portfolio project using Claude API to synthesize 3 public datasets into a consumer insights report
Learn Qualtrics XM platform via free certification

emergingCareers.title: AI Research Specialist
emergingCareers.description: Designs research protocols that combine AI-powered data collection with human analysis to produce market intelligence faster and more cheaply than traditional methods. Manages AI tool selection, prompt engineering for research tasks, and output quality review.

stat.text: 66% of marketers globally are using AI in their roles, with market research and competitive analysis among the top three use cases cited by practitioners.
stat.sourceTitle: HubSpot AI Trends for Marketers Report

emergingCareers.search_query: "AI research specialist" OR "market research AI" analyst jobs 2025
emergingCareers.fit: Analytical rigor and research methodology carry directly; the new skill is designing AI-assisted research workflows that produce reliable, unbiased outputs rather than running all steps manually.
steps: Complete a prompt engineering course (Anthropic or DeepLearning.AI)
Run a side-by-side comparison study: AI-assisted vs. traditional research on the same question
Learn Python basics for data cleaning and automation via Codecademy

emergingCareers.title: Marketing Data Analyst
emergingCareers.description: Builds and maintains dashboards and models that measure marketing performance across AI-assisted campaigns, identifying which outputs drive outcomes and which need human refinement. Acts as the quality control layer between AI-generated marketing activity and business results.

stat.text: Marketing analytics roles with AI tool proficiency command a 15-20% salary premium over traditional marketing analyst roles, per 2024 job posting data.
stat.sourceTitle: The Generative AI Job Market: 2025 Data Insights

emergingCareers.fit: Statistical analysis and report-writing transfer directly; the shift is from describing what happened to modeling which AI-assisted marketing inputs caused business outcomes.
steps: Earn Google Analytics 4 certification
Build a marketing attribution model using publicly available e-commerce data
Complete SQL for Data Analysis course via Mode Analytics or Codecademy

emergingCareers.title: Competitive Intelligence Analyst
emergingCareers.description: Monitors competitor positioning, pricing, and messaging using AI-powered listening tools, then synthesizes findings into strategic briefs for product and go-to-market teams. Owns the system that continuously tracks market shifts using automated AI data collection.

stat.text: 51% of job postings requiring AI skills are now outside IT and computer science -- analyst roles in marketing and strategy are among the fastest-growing categories, with AI skills commanding a 28% salary premium.
stat.sourceTitle: The Generative AI Job Market: 2025 Data Insights

emergingCareers.fit: Competitive analysis and synthesizing market data into narratives carries directly; the new context is owning an always-on AI monitoring system rather than producing periodic manual reports.
steps: Set up a free Crayon or Klue trial and build a competitor tracking dashboard
Complete a course on web scraping and data aggregation via DataCamp
Produce a competitive landscape brief using AI tools and present findings to a stakeholder

careerCluster.title: Budget Analyst
careerCluster.description: Quantitative step up for analysts who want to move into finance functions; requires adding budgeting and variance analysis skills.
careerCluster.fit: Budget modeling and variance analysis provide the quantitative foundation — add forward-looking planning and FP&A tool proficiency.
steps: Earn Anaplan or Workday Adaptive Planning certification
Build a 3-statement financial model as portfolio work
Study financial planning principles through AFP certification preparation

careerCluster.title: Management Analyst
careerCluster.description: Natural progression — broader organizational scope and stronger stakeholder influence built on the same analytical foundation.
careerCluster.fit: Process mapping and stakeholder analysis skills transfer directly — expand scope to organizational strategy and client-facing consulting.
steps: Lead an internal process improvement project and document it as consulting work
Earn a project management certification (PMP or CAPM)
Develop expertise in a specific industry vertical to differentiate as a specialist

careerCluster.title: Financial Examiner
careerCluster.description: Long-horizon path for analysts who move into regulatory careers over time.
careerCluster.fit: Compliance and regulatory knowledge directly supports examiner work — pursuing a regulatory agency position formalizes that expertise into legal authority.
steps: Apply for entry-level examiner positions at OCC, FDIC, or state banking departments
Study bank examination fundamentals through OCC public training materials
Build institutional knowledge in financial regulation through CRCM certification

careerCluster.fit: Consumer insight and campaign measurement skills carry over directly, but Marketing Manager is a step up that requires owning budget, managing a team, and setting strategy rather than supporting it.
steps: Earn a Google Analytics 4 or HubSpot Marketing certification
Lead a cross-functional campaign from brief to post-mortem
Complete an MBA or AMA Professional Certified Marketer program

careerCluster.fit: Data analysis and consumer behavior research translate directly, but Search Marketing Strategist is a step up that requires hands-on paid and organic campaign execution rather than insight delivery.
steps: Earn Google Ads certification (Search + Analytics)
Build a paid search campaign portfolio with real spend data
Complete Google Analytics 4 certification

careerCluster.fit: Report writing and translating data into narrative carry over, but Writers and Authors is a step up in creative autonomy — the work is original content and storytelling rather than research-backed analysis.
steps: Build a writing portfolio across 3 formats (long-form, copy, editorial)
Pitch and publish 2-3 bylined pieces in trade or industry outlets
Complete a content strategy course (Copyblogger or Northwestern Medill)

careerCluster.fit: Consumer and media research skills are directly applicable, but PR Specialist is a lateral move that centers on communications and media relationships rather than data synthesis.
steps: Build a writing portfolio with 3 press releases and a media pitch
Earn PRSA's Communication Management Professional certificate
Shadow or assist a PR team on a campaign in an agency environment

careerCluster.fit: Report writing and fact-verification experience carries over, but Editor is a step up that requires deep editorial judgment and managing other writers' work rather than producing research outputs.
steps: Build a portfolio of edited and published pieces across formats
Complete a copyediting certificate (UC San Diego Extension or ACES)
Contribute as a volunteer editor for an industry publication or newsletter

careerCluster.fit: Market research and campaign measurement skills are directly relevant, but Advertising and Promotions Manager is a step up that requires managing budgets, vendors, and creative teams rather than delivering insights.
steps: Earn AMA's Professional Certified Marketer (PCM) credential
Lead an end-to-end promotional campaign with budget ownership
Build experience managing agency or freelancer relationships

careerCluster.title: Financial Analyst
careerCluster.fit: Quantitative modeling and data analysis skills transfer directly, but Financial Analyst is a lateral move into investment or corporate finance that requires adding domain-specific financial modeling skills.
steps: Complete CFA Level 1 for investment analysis credentials
Learn Python for Finance via DataCamp or Coursera
Build a financial model portfolio with publicly available data
